reviews.review_description: I like it and I hope u can take my comment into consideration I would like you to make a Vietnam game and maybe even a Afghanistan and Iraq one because I'm really into history and now that you got Stalingrad and D-Day I've been playing it ever since it came out both of them actually. Thank you for the kind words and for supporting both Stalingrad and D-Day! 😄

Right now we're working on the German D-Day DLC, and after that we'll be moving on to the Pacific. As for what comes next, we definitely want to keep expanding the Days of Heroes series, so your suggestions have a pretty good chance of becoming reality someday :)

reviews.review_description: I obviously don't know what Stalingrad but i imagine it being what they make it seem... But my experience was like no other, I've been in many wars but this one... It was the worst war along with the worst battle in human history. But this game makes it seem like I'm actually there witch from my words i am saying they made the game so good i thought i was actually there, witch was more good than bad. The bad thing is that, again they made it look so good i thought i was actually there witch is sad because think of all of those poor soldiers. But that makes you one of them... If you die you don't have honor, because honor dies within you

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the kind words! 🙌

One of our main goals was to make players feel like they were actually there, experiencing the scale, chaos, and intensity of the battle. We're glad the atmosphere resonated with you.

Thank you for playing and for taking the time to leave a review! 🫡

reviews.review_title: Very good  game but lacks stability.
reviews.review_description:
The game has a lot of potential and the atmosphere is great but the weapon handling really needs improvement.
Right now the guns are very unstable: they shake a lot, aiming is difficult and holding the barrel or keeping the trigger pressed doesn’t always work. Sometimes, I even have to die and respawn just to get the controls working again.

I also play Onward, so I know it’s not the same style of game but the difference in stability and precision is huge.
If you could improve hand detection, weapon stability, and trigger reliability, the gameplay would feel much smoother.

I’m giving the game 3 stars for now, but if you fix the weapon handling issues, I’m absolutely ready to update my review to 5 stars. The game really deserves it once the controls feel solid.

developer_response.body: The update improving weapon handling has already been implemented. We invite you to check it out and see how the controls feel now — we’d really appreciate your feedback after trying the new version.

reviews.review_description:
I absolutely love this game but a few weeks in I can’t pull back the bolt on a bolt-action anymore… You think I could get my money back so I can get the D-Day?



Alright thanks! I appreciate the heads-up!

developer_response.body:
You're welcome! 😄🫡

And thank you for your patience and understanding. 👍

reviews.review_description:
This game is great but it needs a sequel to it or maybe more missions also some times the place where you put your gun is broken as well as the places to put your medkits and grenades  . It’s a good game but maybe you can have a version where you head to Berlin on the Soviet side.




Ps: I am the same British guy from last time and if you want to more on the eastern side instead of Dunkirk you could do the Chinese and the Japanese and in setting you could add a setting that makes blood go on the floor and not despawn.

developer_response.body:
Thank you very much for the review and the suggestions! 🙌

As for the equipment issues — try adjusting the equipment placement/settings to better fit yourself. It honestly helps a lot with grabbing and storing items comfortably.

And regarding future projects... we'll be announcing what we're working on next fairly soon 😄

reviews.review_description: Maps r too small. The mechanics you were a sweat on but surroundings were too weak. Ong you took inspiration from call of duty. Maybe make the maps bigger like that one snow level. If you need an extra dev i can help update your game. Im making a game rn if u need help 🥹 ill work for free twin.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the feedback 

Higher difficulty levels change the gameplay quite a lot and make the experience much longer and more demanding 

Feel free to join our Discord if you want to share more thoughts with us: www.dohvr.com/discord/

reviews.review_description: The game has too many problems with controls where your gun will fall from hands mid shootout and gameplay is just as terrible you will randomly explode where artillery is unable to go or get one tapped by an MP40 five buildings over and you're fighting your own inventory on the lowest difficulty. 10/10 worst WW2 game I've had ever played

developer_response.body:
We haven’t received any widespread reports from other players regarding controller issues or guns randomly falling out of hands, and we also haven’t been able to reproduce that problem on our side.

As for the MP40 — the AI soldiers definitely do not have the kind of accuracy you described, especially not over those kinds of distances.

The artillery works by covering an area randomly. Sometimes you get lucky and the shells miss you, and sometimes you get unlucky and get hit — that unpredictability is intentional and meant to reflect the chaos of war.

Feel free to join our Discord so we can talk more about it and maybe figure out what exactly happened on your side

www.dohvr.com/discord/

You can already try Limited Ammo Mode in D-Day, as we added it in the latest update.

As for multiplayer, it's definitely something we'd like to explore in the future. Thanks again for your support!

reviews.review_title: I genuinely love this game so much!!
reviews.review_description:
Ever since I saw a TikTok for this game, I knew it was good, I then bought it and it was even better than I ever expected it to be!! This game is one I can always come back to, to improve my skills in the game and just in general enjoy the overall immersive gameplay experience of it itself! This game has also sparked my interest in the battle of Stalingrad itself as I am fascinated by both world wars but more by WW1 and so I don’t really know much about the eastern front in ww2!! reviews.review_description:
I’ve spent several hours in game now, and it’s clear I’ve stumbled upon something special. The foundation you’ve built is incredible
the gun handling feels tactile and responsive, the voice acting is top tier, and the storyline is genuinely engaging. I especially appreciate how user friendly the settings are having that level of customization and difficulty control makes the experience feel very polished.

However, after the initial "wow" factor, the gameplay loop starts to feel a bit repetitive due to a lack of content variety. To really take this to the next level, I’d love to see an expansion of the combat mechanics. Right now, we are limited to a couple of knives, but the world would feel much more alive with "found" melee weapons like fire axes, wrenches, glass bottles, or lead pipes. It would also be a game changer to have a physical interaction system where you can grab, hold, or throw NPCs to clearing a path or knocking them down.

Building on that, the enemy variety could use some tuning. Currently, almost every enemy is armed with a gun, which makes encounters feel a bit one dimensional. Introducing melee focused enemies would force players to switch up their tactics. On the immersion side, moving toward full body avatars instead of floating hands would be a massive upgrade. It’s a small visual change that makes a huge difference in how "present" you feel in the world.

Finally, I think the game would benefit immensely from a true Sandbox Mode. While the current range is a good start, it’s not truly limitless. A mode where we can select a map and freely spawn props and NPCs would provide endless replayability and let players experiment with the mechanics you’ve worked so hard on.

I am more than happy to update my review to 5 stars as the game evolves. I truly believe in this project and would even be interested in a supporter DLC or add on to help fund these features. Keep up the great work!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for such a detailed and thoughtful review — we really appreciate it 🙌

We’re really glad you’re enjoying the core gameplay and overall experience. You brought up some great points about variety and replayability — that kind of feedback helps us a lot.

Right now we’re focused on the D-Day remaster (already in the testing phase), which is a natural next step for the series and where we’re pushing a lot of improvements forward.

Stalingrad has also received many updates since release, and we’re still keeping an eye on it as well.

Thanks again for the support — it really means a lot to us!

reviews.review_title: I love it and the d-day one  but...
reviews.review_description: I think you need to add a vietnam one to the pack and other war based campain games like gor example "Days of heroes Vietnam"

developer_response.body:
Hey, thank you so much for the great feedback, really appreciate it! 😄🔥
If you enjoyed D-Day, we’ve got good news — we’ve been working on a full remaster for months now, and it will come as a free update. We’re already in the testing phase, so it’s coming soon!

And as for Days of Heroes: Vietnam — yeah… that one just has to happen 😄 Thanks again!

reviews.review_description: Way better than D-Day, especially in terms of gameplay and functionality. Still has noticeable bugs, though. Maybe we can have a Day of Heroes game set in Japan, North Africa, or outside of WWII altogether. Silent death mission sucks though It gives no clue of where the enemy sniper is

developer_response.body:
Appreciate the feedback, but dropping the whole rating because of one mission feels a bit harsh, don’t you think?

The captain actually calls out the sniper’s position — but it’s designed as a sniper duel, so you’re not supposed to spot him instantly. Still, we’ll look into making the hints clearer 👍

reviews.review_title: Good game but could be cheaper
reviews.review_description:
I have enjoyed this game like the artillery and the mortars and tanks. The missions can seem a little repetitive later on but they are good. This is the first really good ww2 game and has made me impressed. The game is a decent priced game. The game doesn’t have much replayability.

If I had to rate parts of the game

Maps 8/10
Story telling 9/10
Ending 9/10
Features(tanks, artillery) 10/10
Quality of life 8/10
Guns 7/10
Price 6.5/10

Overall a good game they did good on the cool features but the price could be little better

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the updated review 👍
Glad you enjoyed the game and that the recent changes improved your experience. Appreciate the feedback and the time you spent with the game 💪

reviews.review_description:
First up, I need to calibrate. There is no in-game crouch or crawl option, right? Yet one really needs to crouch behind cover.  Sometimes it may even be better to  crawl. German mission #4 where there is a Soviet sniper, I finally started crawling. This means literally lying down on my gaming room floor, wishing I had a rug instead of just hard flooring. Fine fine, the game is good enough, this is normal, right?

First game ever to get me to actually lie face down on the floor and remember what it felt like to do the same playing paintball.

Gunplay is just superb. Comparable to the best I have ever experienced in VR. A lot like The Light Brigade, a AAA game by comparison. 

Voice acting is authentic. Funny. Not camp. Just funny, especially when the speakers appear within an actual level and you can follow and listen to them. 

Incredible high value game. I'll be buying the D-Day version too.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for this amazing and thoughtful comment — it genuinely put a smile on our faces 🙂

Great news: we actually released an update just yesterday that adds a crouch option. 

You can enable crouching by:

opening Settings using the Menu button on the left controller

going to the last tab, where you’ll find the crouch option

We’re really happy you’re enjoying the experience, and we wish you a great time playing!

Also if yall haven’t saw this game got coverage on this site 

strategyandwargaming dot com had this game on Chris top 2025  games . Can’t submit the link on here though.

If you’d like to share any bugs or issues you ran into, feel free to join our Discord and let us know — it really helps us improve the game: www.dohvr.com/discord/

I would like to start by saying what an amazing game and experience DAY OF HEROES STALINGRAD is .
I thought you did a marvelous job with DAYS OF HEROES D DAY, but you just surpassed yourself with DAYS OF HEROES STALINGRAD, the V.R exsperiance is amazing and as someone who loved  pc ww2 Sims and strategy games this fits nicely in there on the meta quest 3.

We’re currently working hard on the revamped Days of Heroes: D-Day, and we hope you’ll enjoy what’s coming next. We also hope to bring more content and new campaigns in the future.

The update is taking quite a while because it’s essentially a full rebuild — we’re reworking the game almost from the ground up to make it significantly better.

reviews.review_description: I mean, dont get me wrong, this was a good game, but every mission was way too short for a $20 dollar game and your going to have mortars and artillery guns but only have them in like 4 missions that are 3~5 minutes long. Good but good be way better

developer_response.body:
Thanks for your feedback! Just to clarify, the game costs $15.99, not $20.

Also, if you can complete missions in 3–5 minutes, it might mean you’re playing on a lower difficulty. The Easy level is designed for players who are new to VR.

For context, there aren’t actually missions that short — the early missions, like the Volga crossing or the assault on the trenches, are meant as an introduction. They might feel short at first, but think of them as a cinematic prologue to the main experience.

Hope that helps clear things up!

reviews.review_description: This is one of those games were the style of graphics does not matter one bit. The gameplay is so great and immersive you forget all about it. reviews.review_description:
This is great, I mean two games in one, how good is that, complete as the Russians and play again from different perspective as the Germans, that’s cool in itself.
The game is different with more basic polygon graphics but that does not affect it in the wrong way at all. I am really enjoying it, even though I am only part way in I am really looking forward to completing it from both forces perspective….  
The game play sells this, whether you are in a battle in the middle of the streets looking for cover or sat in a tank it’s amazing.  This is one you want for your VR library.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the 5/5 review and your support! 🙌

reviews.review_description:
This is interesting.
Polygon graphics, which are very good.
Sounds are good enough.
You play missions as either the Russians or Germans.

I like the immersion you get from the battles, especially the tank missions… but the reloading is a bit janky and makes it awkward. Also the parts inside the tanks are awkward with the aiming the turrets… especially rotating it to aim.
But i really do like being inside the tanks… aiming turret, reloading the shells into the turret, shooting the machine gun and also putting out fires inside the tanks, using the fire extinguisher.

The levels are quite tough when on foot, the enemy AI is decent enough to make the levels a challenge.

This is an enjoyable fps game if you can look past some of the janky reloading and tank aiming.
If they can improve the loading and tank aiming, this would make it a way better game.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for taking the time to share your thoughts!
We’re really glad you enjoyed the immersion and especially the tank experience — it’s a big part of what we wanted to achieve with the game.

Community feedback is a very important for us. If you ever have specific ideas or suggestions, you’re very welcome to join our Discord and share them with us there — we actively read and discuss player feedback.

(www.dohvr.com/discord/)

Appreciate you playing and giving the game a chance! 💪

reviews.review_description:
This is one of those rare games that comes along and it’s an amazing find.
Don’t be fooled by the low polygon graphics, this game is sensational, gameplay is just so spot on.
Love all the world war 2 guns, they all perform well, story is so immersive  and you get a real feel for the war.
Giant explosions, gratifying head shots just make me want to just to keep playing and playing.
Bravo devs, amazing job.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the kind words and support! 🙌
It really means a lot to us — comments like this motivate us to keep improving and creating more content for you. We’re glad you’re enjoying the game! 💪

reviews.review_description:
The graphics are polygonal, yet the controls and content feel real, making it a genuine VR shooter.
In fact, the polygonal nature seems to heighten the horror of war.
In VR games, I believe comfortable gameplay elements like controls and physics simulation are far more important than high-end graphics. That's why I give this VR game an unquestionable five stars.
If anyone is misjudging this game as lacking substance based on its graphics, I urge you to experience it for yourself. Fortunately, a demo version is available. Trying it will show you just how outstanding a VR game this truly is.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for this incredible review,  supporting us and for encouraging others to try the demo and judge the game for themselves. Comments like yours give us huge energy to keep going! 💪

reviews.review_description: Such a fun game to play. I already own the D-Day one and won a code for this one. I prefer this one for some reason.

developer_response.body: Glad you enjoy it

reviews.review_description: This is extremely good. I played this one and the first game and both were extremely fun. However I wish there were more tank missions. Or for your next game I would suggest making a whole game about being in tanks and doing tank things. There are barely any tank games on quest so there’s quite a bit of opportunity there. Anyway this is a great game

developer_response.body: We're glad you had fun :) Regarding the tanks, I think you're right and they deserve more attention. Stalingrad is more of a test/teaser for it and we didn't want to overdo it in case someone didn't like it. We're only getting positive feedback so far, so I think it's the right direction. We're currently working on the D-Day update (where we'll also add new tank missions). Join us on Discord to stay up to date: https://dohvr.com/discord/

reviews.review_description:
Buy this game ASAP.
If you are a ww2 aficionado, then this is most definitely the best game for you on vr right now (and yes it is even better than Medal of Honor VR.) being able to fight on both sides is a privilege rarely seen in the games of today, and it is remarkably satisfying for your main objective to ultimately lose the battle. My favourite part of the entire game — I think — is the field cannons and their remarkably smooth animations.

Even though it has a few minor bugs and glitches and things like that, it is still 100% worth  $16.

I would love a ww1 Days of Heroes game. 

And in the meantime, I just want to thank the devs for the awesomeness that they have bred into the world.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for this amazing feedback — it really makes our day! 🙏
We’re already working on an update and hope it will iron out the bugs and glitches you mentioned.

Huge thanks again for supporting our work and sharing such kind words! ❤️

reviews.review_description: This had to be the worst game I've played on vr the guns was stupidly laggy an the game was extremely buggy

developer_response.body:
Could you please join our Discord and tell us exactly what was buggy and what didn’t work with the guns?
We currently have no reports of issues like this, and on our side everything seems to be working normally – so your feedback would really help us understand what’s going on.

👉 www.dohvr.com/discord/

We’ll gladly look into it and try to fix the problem

Hey! We’re still waiting to hear what exactly isn’t working for you. We’d really like to fix any bugs so the gameplay feels great for you. Let us know what you’ve noticed 

If you don't use Discord, please email us at zenappsstudio@gmail.com

reviews.review_description: I actually like the look and it reminds me a lot of the five-part Cactus Cowboy games, which are true masterpieces. But this is where the difference lies: Days of Heroes is mostly unfair and the missions vary in length (sometimes youre just playing a cutscene or the mission is 30 seconds long). The worst part is that you dive into a mission and often just get shot without being able to influence it. In one mission, you don't even have a weapon and it's almost impossible to pick up a friend's weapon, and when you're close to it, it simply disappears. The game is endlessly frustrating and a return doesn't seem possible either, as all attempts have been rejected. A complete waste of money! And judging by the other reviews, these players were clearly playing a completely different game.

developer_response.body:
We know Cactus Cowboy and we agree it’s a great game — but it’s also a very different experience with a completely different atmosphere and design goals.
We understand your reaction, and we’re sorry if this wasn’t what you expected. But we don’t agree that Days of Heroes deserves such a low rating. Do missions vary in length? Yes — but that depends on your skill, difficulty setting, and sometimes randomness.
There are two shorter “cinematic” missions that serve as a prologue to the Soviet campaign. These are based on iconic historical events like the crossing of the Volga River and the famous “Not One Step Back” order. We deliberately included them because they reflect the historical background and atmosphere of the battle.
As for the level where you don’t immediately get a weapon — that was a conscious design decision, inspired by movies like Enemy at the Gates. We wanted players to feel the chaos and desperation of being thrown into the front lines.
Throughout the game, we tried to ground weapons, locations, and events in history — for example, the battle for the railway station in the heart of Stalingrad. Of course, it’s not a 100% simulation of historical reality — it’s influenced by the more “romanticized” depictions of the battle you see in films.

We understand the game won’t be for everyone, but calling it a “30-second cutscene” or “unplayable” simply isn’t fair.

We encourage you to play more of the campaign before making a final judgment — and if you’d like to share your perspective in more detail, we’d love to discuss it with you on our Discord. Feedback from players who aren’t as deep into the history or films behind our inspirations is very valuable to us. Together, we can make the game even better.
👉 http://dohvr.com/discord

reviews.review_description: This is a really good game sorry for accusing you🙏

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much — you have no idea how much this means to us.
We really appreciate that you took the time to update your comment. 🙏

We’re just a small 2-person team, putting our whole heart and passion into this game, so helping players and fixing issues like this is incredibly important to us.
We’re genuinely happy that we were able to help and that everything is working now.

Have an amazing time playing — and if you ever want to talk or share feedback, feel free to join our Discord!
www.dohvr.com/discord/
 🎮💙

reviews.review_description: You must play it it’s so fun! Ever since I got it I’ve been playing all the time. I love it!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much! We're really glad you're enjoying it — that means a lot to us!

reviews.review_title: One of the best games I've  played
reviews.review_description: I've played many games this is one of the best i love this and the d day one there awesome but please make a ww1 game or napoleonic fighting type of game it would be awsome

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much, that really means a lot to us! ❤️
We’re super happy you enjoy both Stalingrad and D-Day.

As for your suggestion — a game set in World War I is something we have in our plans and ideas for the future.
For now, we’re fully focused on finishing the Major Update for Days of Heroes: D-Day.

Thanks for the support and for sharing your ideas with us!

reviews.review_description:
Days of Heroes Stalingrad offers Two campaigns on The eastern front. The missions are immerive and very fun, you get to fight in ruined Cities capturing key places like railstations and factories. While at The same Time moving down people with guns, The reloading IS very satisfying too. My end conclusion IS 4.7/5 definetly The best pick for 15$ to someone who enjoys some good WWII VR Shooter title
        
Thank you Zenapps Studio for this amazing experience
       🫡

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much! 🙌
We're truly honored — comments like this mean the world to us.
We’ve put our hearts into making Stalingrad something special, and hearing it’s your favorite WW2 VR shooter is the best reward we could ask for.

See you on the front lines, soldier! 🎖️🔥

reviews.review_description: The game reloading and combat is amazing. The missions are really good aswell as the fact that you can play on both sides of the war which is a cool feature. Overall a good game even for those who aren’t intressted in war type things.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the positive feedback — it truly means a lot to us! Every kind comment gives us the energy to keep pushing forward. We’re really happy to hear that you enjoyed Days of Heroes: Stalingrad. Thanks again!

reviews.review_title: Hey I love this game man it’s so fun
reviews.review_description: Also what missions have the tanks? Although this game is super fun not much graphics but better then above and beyond super fun bro

developer_response.body:
Hey! Thank you so much — we’re really glad you’re having fun with the game! 🙏
If you want to jump into tank missions, check out the German campaign — missions “Panzer Strike” and “Tank Struggles”

And thanks for the kind words — it really means a lot to us that you rank our game above such big titles, especially since we’re just a small two-person team working hard to make something special! 💪

reviews.review_description:
After playing the game for a while, I noticed that it is fundamentally different (in a good way) from Days of Heroes: Day-D. There are now physics of items, you can pick up weapons from your teammates if they are dead etc. Even the German flag was changed and (now I quoting my comment from the developers' discord channel) "You know, I really like the new symbol, even though the boot was funnier". I honestly had to re-adapt to the game 😅. 
In conclusion, I can only say one thing:

Iron Cross II — for the idea.

Iron Cross I — for the desire to continue the franchise "Days of Heroes".

Pour le Mérite (Blue Max) — for the work done.

Bravo, Zen Apps Studio studio! BRAVO! 🙌

developer_response.body:
Wow — thank you so much for this incredible comment! 🙏🔥

We’re truly happy to hear that you noticed and appreciated all the changes and improvements we’ve worked so hard on. Physics, new mechanics, small details — all of it was added to push Stalingrad a step forward. We're also glad you like the new symbol (even if the boot had its own charm 😄).

Feedback like this gives us the energy to keep building and growing the Days of Heroes franchise.

Thank you — and message received, soldier! 🎖️💪

reviews.review_description: After the campaign it suked can i get a refund please

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the feedback.
However, a few things need to be clarified:

1️⃣ If the entire campaign was completed, it clearly means the game delivered enough engagement to play it through from start to finish. A 2/5 rating doesn’t match that experience.

2️⃣ The game is exactly what we describe on the store page — a story-driven campaign with a specific number of missions. All of that is communicated clearly before purchase.

3️⃣ Every player has the option to request a refund directly through the platform before completing the game, if they feel it’s not for them.
If a refund was not initiated, it usually means the purchase was considered satisfactory at the time.

We always welcome fair feedback, but this comment reads more like an attempt to use a negative rating as leverage rather than genuine criticism.
If you have specific, constructive notes — we’re always open to hearing them.

reviews.review_description:
In November of 2022, I was playing the demo of the D-Day game every day. I loved it so much that the developers ended up gifting me the full version, something I’ll never forget.
This new game is even better than the last. The story is really good, and as someone who loves history, I found it very fun. If you’re into history too, I absolutely recommend buying this game.
One of my favorite parts is that you can choose which side to play on either the Third Reich or the Soviet Union which gives each playthrough a unique perspective. There are many battles to fight through, and they all are really fun.

I want to personally thank the developers, not only for their kindness but for creating such a great experience.

developer_response.body:
Wow, thank you so much for such an amazing message! 💛
It really means a lot to us that you’ve been with us since the D‑Day and that you’ve enjoyed both games. Knowing that someone who loves history appreciates the story and the different perspectives we’ve built in is the best reward we could ask for.

We’re truly grateful for your support and your kind words — it motivates us to keep creating and improving. Enjoy every battle out there! 🔥🎮

reviews.review_description: I loved both Stalingrad and D-Day so much I think they should have one about the pacific or italy or vietnam just some ideas          : )

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your positive comment — it really means a lot to us! 🙏
The first good news is that we’ve already started working on a big update for D-Day to bring it closer in quality to Stalingrad.
As for the Pacific — we’d love to make that happen one day, and if there’s enough interest in our games, it could be the next step for the series. ⚔️

Once again, thank you for the support!

reviews.review_description: May be low quality but very good game

developer_response.body: Thank you for the 5/5 rating! We’re glad you enjoyed the game

reviews.review_title: A Must-Have Journey Through WWII History
reviews.review_description:
Days of Heroes: Stalingrad is a fantastic VR title for several reasons. The execution is solid, the atmosphere is immersive, and the story progression is excellent, with a script that pulls you straight into the intensity of wartime.

What makes this game truly stand out is its historical authenticity. It's one of the rare VR titles that lets you relive key events from WWII—not only from the Russian perspective during the Stalin era but also from the German side. Each chapter focuses on a significant moment in history, offering a powerful learning experience while placing you directly in the boots of a soldier.

The missions are varied and perfectly paced—not too long, but packed with meaning. Whether you're into history or looking for an emotionally engaging VR experience, this is an unmissable title in any serious VR collection.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the kind words! 😊 We’re really glad you enjoyed the game and found the way we presented the history engaging. We aimed to strike the right balance between gameplay and historical atmosphere, so it’s truly encouraging to hear that you appreciate that. Thanks for taking the time to share your great feedback!

reviews.review_description: Great game! I was already a fan of the first DOH D-Day! And after playing this one, I’m incredibly surprised by all the improvements! I’ll never get tired of playing this new version! I’d love to see the D-Day version updated with all the new features from Stalingrad!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much! 🙌 We're really glad you’re enjoying the improvements in Stalingrad! And yes — updating D-Day is exactly what we’re planning next 👀 Thank you for being with us since the beginning! 💥

i love the game btw

reviews.review_description: Not the most historically accurate however it's pure fun and I love how it's call of duty 2

developer_response.body:
Exactly 😄 You nailed it.

We were heavily inspired by Call of Duty 2 — it was one of the games of our childhood, and we spent hundreds of hours playing it. Movies like Enemy at the Gates were also a big influence on the atmosphere and pacing.

Thanks a lot for the 5/5 rating and the kind words.
We’re glad you’re having fun — and we wish you a great time playing! 🎮🔥

reviews.review_description:
Great game I’ve played every demo and full game of days of heroes but this one is just a masterpiece makes me wanna cry happy tears because of how good it is but there is a couple things that should be added like more gore and better ai other than that it’s amazing I hope there are more games but they take place in ww1 that would be amazing anyway keep up the great work guys 


                                              Love- roostersoldier013

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback, we really appreciate it. The next update will be dedicated to improving AI targeting, so that's another step towards improving our game – so you're right :) When you have a moment, please join us on our Discord at www.dohvr.com/discord where we collect feedback and strive to improve it. reviews.review_description: Despite the polygonal models, the gameplay is very well thought out, both the plot and the functionality during the battles - very diverse game mechanics, and driving on military equipment and shooting with heavy weapons, there are a couple of moments of inconvenience with the game sitting/standing - but otherwise the game is very worthy!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your thoughtful feedback! 🙌
We’re really glad you’re enjoying the gameplay, mechanics, and all the variety we’ve packed in. 💪🔥
Your kind words mean a lot to us — thanks for playing! 🎮🫡

reviews.review_description:
This game feels much larger than Day of Heroes….everything from mechanics to physics to graphics are improved. It’s a fun and silly game, not realistic in any way really, but that’s its charm. These are the kind of devs I love supporting. Need more “Zen Apps Studios” type developers dedicated to quest games. 
One minor complaint which is supposedly getting a fix in a day or 2, is right now there’s no haptic feedback while shooting.  I wanna feel the rumble every time I pull the trigger lol. Also needs faster Smooth Turning. Again, very minor complaint. All in all, this is a very polished game

One last thing I can’t forget to mention: these devs are VERY active on their discord. Every question/critique was promptly addressed within a few hours of posting, sometimes within minutes. Can’t beat that kind of customer service. Highly recommend you join their discord.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the kind words and for helping us improve our game!
We’re really happy you’re enjoying the game and that you noticed the improvements we’ve been working on. It means a lot to us that you appreciate our approach and the effort we put into the project.

Your support and encouragement keep us going – thanks for being part of our community! 💛

reviews.review_description:
Great game i like the new hand stuff  but 
needs more weapons

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the feedback 🙏 We know how much players enjoy tank missions, we’ll definitely keep this in mind moving forward

reviews.review_title: Enemy at the Gates in VR
reviews.review_description: Been waiting for this for so long, it’s been delayed to May, then June, then finally July. And it’s finally here And it’s SO Worth the wait! The references from COD 2, WAW, Vanguard and Enemy at the Gates. And the amazing gunplay all in the eastern front is just chefs kiss . Great job Zen Games! Keep on doing this! (Days of Heroes Peleliu next?!)

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your amazing words! 🙏 We’re thrilled that you noticed all those references and enjoyed the gunplay on the Eastern Front. 💪
We also want to say that we truly apologize for the delays — we beat ourselves up over them — but we really wanted to make sure Stalingrad was as polished as possible.

And yes… we definitely have dreams for the next chapter — and let’s just say, you’re pointing in a very interesting direction. 😉🔥

reviews.review_description: I got the first game but the second game was two times better. Although I said this before I’m going to say it again, it needs co-op. This game would be so much better with friends.

developer_response.body:
Totally agree with you on co‑op — playing with friends would take it to a whole new level! 👥🔥
It’s definitely on our radar, so thank you for bringing it up again and for all the support!
